Dark heads rise and vanish among the waves beside the hull. The raiders climb with hooks when spray hides the first splash.
A slick of blood from some unseen kill trails across the swell. Reef sharks follow it toward anything thrashing in the water.
A heavy thump rolls under the boat as thick arms curl around the keel. The octopus squeezes from below while the sea rocks above.
The sea lies smooth as hammered metal, but low ripples cross against the wind. Reading them reveals the start of a dangerous current.
A single wave rises from the wrong direction and slams hard across the deck or rocks. Loose gear and loose footing both become hazards.
At night the wake glows green with sparks of living light. Each oar stroke or wave slap scatters stars through the black water.
A torn signal flag floats beside a patch of wreckage. Its pattern marks distress, but the nearest ship is nowhere in sight.
A sealed bottle bobs in a tangle of weed, its message written in a hand shaking from haste. The coordinates point off the known route.
A warning buoy drifts far from its anchor, its bell packed with seaweed. Resetting it could keep the next ship off the shoals.
A wide mat of kelp rolls beside the current, sheltering tiny crabs and edible fronds. The freshest blades sit near the center.
Air-filled weed carries little berry-like bladders prized for bitter stews. Sorting them before they burst takes a light touch.
A cluster of coconuts and palm debris floats in the wake of a distant storm. The good nuts knock hollow and low.
A school of mackerel breaks the surface in silver bursts. Fast lines can bring in several before larger hunters scatter them.
Diving birds mark a tuna run just ahead of the swell. Reaching it in time means reading the flock more than the water.
An old lobster pot drags along a shallow reef, still baited and still catching. Recovering it risks scraping hull or boots on coral.
A raft of pale pumice stones drifts across the swell after a distant eruption. The light stones can be scooped before they spread too thin.
A shallow shoal glimmers with oyster shells below clear water. Taking pearls without tearing the bed apart requires care.
A waxy gray lump floats among gull feathers and weed. Identifying it before the sea takes it again turns refuse into value.
A broken mast drifts just below the surface, dangerous to hulls but useful if hauled aboard. Its rigging tangles around everything nearby.
Wreckage from a shattered deck rides the swell in a loose fan. The longest planks can be hooked before the current spreads them out.
Long kelp stems twist together like rope beneath a floating mat. Properly dried, they make strong lashings for temporary repairs.
